---
title: "FIT data"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{FIT data}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(rmb)
```

This vignette provides a compact exploratory analysis and an example model for `fit`.

## Study design

```{r study-design}
data(rmb_datasets, package = "rmb")
rmb_datasets$study_design[rmb_datasets$object == "fit"]
```


```{r load-fit}
data(fit, package = "rmb")
dat <- fit
dim(dat)
head(dat)
summary(dat)
```

## Variable labels

```{r labels}
var_labels <- vapply(dat, function(x) {
  lbl <- attr(x, "label")
  if (is.null(lbl) || length(lbl) == 0 || all(lbl == "")) NA_character_ else paste(as.character(lbl), collapse = "; ")
}, character(1))
stats::setNames(var_labels, names(dat))
```

## Example model

```{r model}
num_vars <- names(dat)[vapply(dat, is.numeric, logical(1))]
if (length(num_vars) >= 2) {
  response <- num_vars[1]
  predictor <- num_vars[2]
  df <- stats::na.omit(dat[, c(response, predictor), drop = FALSE])
  if (nrow(df) >= 5) {
    y <- df[[response]]
    if (all(y %in% c(0, 1))) {
      fit <- stats::glm(stats::as.formula(paste(response, predictor, sep = " ~ ")), data = df, family = stats::binomial())
    } else {
      fit <- stats::lm(stats::as.formula(paste(response, predictor, sep = " ~ ")), data = df)
    }
    summary(fit)
  } else {
    cat("Not enough complete observations for a model.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("No two numeric variables available for a simple model example.\n")
}
```

## Source

- UCSF Regression Methods companion data: https://regression.ucsf.edu/sites/g/files/tkssra16191/files/wysiwyg/home/data/fit.dta
- Book: Vittinghoff E, Glidden DV, Shiboski SC, McCulloch CE (2012). *Regression Methods in Biostatistics* (2nd edition).
